landing.heroSub: An AI coach explains <0>every decision in real time</0> — equity, pot odds, opponent range. Animated Range Vision, simulation that <1>proves</1> your edge, cash & tournament training. Become the player others fear.
landing.ctaNote: No credit card · free to start
landing.hook1Desc: On every hand, the coach explains the +EV move: equity, pot odds, opponent range. You understand why — and you improve fast.
landing.hook2Desc: Watch the opponent's range tighten action by action. An animated range read you won't find anywhere else.
landing.hook3Desc: The simulation bench runs thousands of tournaments to PROVE, with hard numbers, that the coach's decisions win.
landing.featuresSub: One tool, from beginner to grinder.
landing.fCash: Play cash with a live coach.
landing.fSim: Measure EV over thousands of hands.
landing.pricingTitle: Start free. Go Pro whenever you want.
landing.finalSub: Join the community and let the coach level you up.
landing.footerDisclaimer: Poker training tool — no real-money gambling, no monetary winnings.

sim.title: Simulation — the coach's test bench
sim.desc: The coach plays tournaments with exactly the same decisions as the autopilot, against bots. We compare it to an average player (a bot in its place) to measure its edge — places, ITM, ROI.
sim.legendMtt: The more mass leans LEFT (high places), the better.
sim.verdictMid: the coach runs a {{r}}% ROI vs {{base}}% for an average player in its place →
sim.verdictBeat: it clearly beats the field by {{pts}} ROI points.
sim.verdictNoEdge: no measurable edge on this sample.
sim.calibNote: (Places CALIBRATED on survival: the average player comes out at ~0% ROI / {{n}}% ITM — so the coach's ABSOLUTE numbers are reliable, not just the gap.)

spotread.title: Spot reading
spotread.intro: Diagnose each hand like a pro: texture → range → equity → bucket. 4 questions per spot.

spotq.textureSemi: Semi-wet — 2 of a suit or one connector
spotq.bucketValue: VALUE — I beat their calling range
spotq.bucketBluffcatch: BLUFF-CATCH — I beat their bluffs, not their value
spotq.bucketDraw: DRAW — behind, but I can hit
spotq.promptTexture: How would you describe the texture of the flop {{board}}?
spotq.promptRange: {{story}} On this flop {{board}}, the range advantage goes to whom?
spotq.promptEquity_one: Against {{count}} opponent on {{board}}, your approximate equity?
spotq.promptEquity_other: Against {{count}} opponents on {{board}}, your approximate equity?
spotq.promptBucket: Your hand {{key}} on {{board}} — which bucket are you in?
spotq.explainTextureDry: Rainbow and disconnected board: few possible draws → ranges don't "connect" much, equity is mostly in made pairs.
spotq.explainTextureWet: Two of a suit AND connected cards: many flush/straight draws → equities are close, made hands must protect.
spotq.explainTexturePaired: Paired board: watch out for trips/full houses, and "two pair" leaning on the board pair is really just one pair.
spotq.explainTextureSemi: Intermediate texture: one connector or two of a suit → a few draws, without being soaked.
spotq.whoCaller: the opponent is the raiser, YOU called
spotq.rangeSideHigh: A {{card}}-high board sticks to the RAISER's range (big Aces/Kings, broadways, big pairs).
spotq.rangeSideLow: A low board (9-high or less) sticks to the CALLER's range (connectors, small pairs, suited) — the raiser mostly has missed high cards.
spotq.rangeSideMid: A T/J-high board with no Ace or King is contested: both ranges hit it about equally → neutral.
spotq.rangeConclHero: → the advantage goes to YOUR range: you can bet/attack more often.
spotq.rangeConclVillain: → the advantage goes to THEIR range: careful, defend without overplaying.
spotq.rangeConclNeutral: → shared advantage: nobody dominates, play tight.
spotq.equityExplain: Your real equity ≈ {{eq}} (simulation vs their range). {{detail}}
spotq.equityValue: Strong made hand → you dominate a big part of their range.
spotq.equityDraw: You're behind now, your equity comes from your draw (you hit ~1 in 3).
spotq.equityBluffcatch: A medium pair: you beat their bluffs/misses but lose vs their made hands → medium equity.
spotq.equityAir: Almost nothing made: your equity mostly comes from overcards / a bit of showdown value.
spotq.bucketExplainValue: {{made}}: you beat their CALLING range → this is VALUE, you bet/raise to get paid by worse. Key question: "do worse hands pay me?" → yes.
spotq.bucketExplainBluffcatch: {{made}}: you beat their bluffs but lose to their value, and you don't improve → BLUFF-CATCH. You check/call, you DON'T bet (worse folds, better calls).
spotq.bucketExplainDraw: {{made}}: behind now, but a draw that can hit → DRAW. You play by the odds + implied odds, or as a semi-bluff.
spotq.bucketExplainAir: {{made}}: no useful pair nor draw → AIR. Either you give up, or you bluff if the board and opponent allow it.
spotq.storyDefend: {{opener}} opens in {{pos}}, you call in the BB{{multiway}}.

decision.title: The right move
decision.intro: Facing the opponent's action (their bet size), pick the right move: fold / call / raise / bet / check. The coach then reveals their range, your two golden questions, and the sizing tell.
decision.promptBet: The opponent BETS {{bb}}bb (~{{pct}}% of the pot). Your turn.
decision.promptCheck: The opponent CHECKS. It's on you (nobody bet).

decq.lessonFold: Not enough equity ({{eq}}) or dominated hand → fold.
decq.lessonCallBc: Bluff-catch: you beat their bluffs, you lose vs their value, and you don't improve → you CALL, you don't raise.
decq.lessonCallDraw: Draw: the odds + implied odds make the call profitable.
decq.lessonCall: Your equity ({{eq}}) beats the odds → call.
decq.lessonRaise: You dominate their calling range → raise to get paid by WORSE.
decq.lessonBetBluff: No showdown value, but the board/passivity let you represent the nuts → bluff (better hands fold).
decq.lessonBetValue: Strong hand: worse hands pay → you bet for value{{prot}}.
decq.lessonCheck: Neither clear value (worse won't pay) nor profitable bluff → check (control / showdown).
decq.twoFold: You beat neither their value nor enough of their bluffs to call → you give up.
decq.twoCallBc: "Do worse hands pay?" No → you don't bet/raise. But you beat their bluffs → you call.
decq.twoRaise: "Do worse hands pay?" YES → you raise for value.
decq.twoBetBluff: "Do better hands fold?" YES → profitable bluff.
decq.twoBetValue: "Do worse hands pay?" YES → value bet.
decq.twoCheck: "Do worse hands pay?" No. "Do better hands fold?" No. → check.
decq.sizeSmall: Sizing tell: they bet SMALL (~{{pct}}% pot) → their range leans weak (capped) → you can continue/attack wider.
decq.sizeBig: Sizing tell: big bet (~pot+) → polarized range (very strong OR bluff) → careful, it's rarely medium.
decq.sizeMid: Sizing tell: medium bet (~{{pct}}% pot) → balanced range.

coach.title: Coach Assistant
coach.hoverHint: hover — leave the profile to close
coach.dominatedFlush: ⚠︎ dominated flush: it's still an out (counted in your equity), but you can hit and still lose to a higher flush.
coach.qEquityCalc: How do you compute my equity?
coach.qPotoddsCalc: How do you compute the odds?
coach.qBluff: What if I try to bluff?
coach.faceRaiseTitle: Plan if the opponent bets / raises you, by size
coach.faceRaiseDisabled: Available when the advice is CHECK, BET or CALL
coach.footerPreflop: Range adapted to the number of players in the hand + equity estimated by simulation. Training advice, not a solver.
coach.footerPostflop: Equity estimated by simulation (Monte-Carlo) vs random hands. Heuristic training advice, not a solver.

hand.madeBoardOnly: High card: {{hi}} (+ the board pair, shared)

cadv.eqVsCapped: — the opponent bet then checked: their range is capped/weak
cadv.potOdds: Pot odds: you need {{odds}} equity to call {{toCall}}.
cadv.capped: CAPPED range: the opponent checked an earlier street — they'd have bet their strong hands before. Their delayed bet is therefore rarely the nuts (more bluffs / medium hands) → you can bluff-catch wider.
cadv.donkLead: Opponent LEAD: it's NOT the preflop raiser betting — a player who flats then LEADS/barrels (often out of position in the field) rarely does it with air. Their range is value-heavy (top pair+/overpair/sets) → your marginal pair is dominated more often, be careful (fold sooner than a classic bluff-catch).
cadv.facingRaise: You're facing a RAISE: it's the STRONGEST signal in poker. People almost never raise a plain top pair — their range is concentrated on two pair / sets / nuts (+ a few draws). Your pair (even top pair) is often BEHIND → don't stack off one pair facing a raise, fold unless you have a truly strong hand or the odds for a draw.
cadv.handBoard: Your hand: {{what}} — nothing of your own{{draws}}.
cadv.handLeanTwoPair: Your hand: top pair ({{pair}}) — your "two pair" leans on the board's pair (shared by everyone), play it as ONE pair.
cadv.oop: You're out of position: be more careful.
cadv.highCardValue: Your "high card" isn't nothing: your equity comes from your overcards (you can pair up), backup draws, and you beat their missed hands/bluffs at showdown.
cadv.sprCommitted: committed — with a strong hand, go all-in.
cadv.sprLow: fairly low, ready to invest big with a strong hand.
cadv.beatenMultiway: ⚠️ Multiway, and several opponents have CALLED multiple streets: calling that much multiway = STRENGTH (top pair+/two pair/sets), not air. Your pair ({{eq}}) is often BEHIND at least one of them → control the pot, don't overvalue / don't stack off one pair.
cadv.valueRaise: Strong hand: you dominate a good part of their range → raise for value.
cadv.bluffCatchBoard: ⚠️ A {{tex}} is possible that you DON'T have: your {{name}} is a BLUFF-CATCHER here. Raising would chase away their bluffs (which you beat) and only get called by their {{tex2}}/hands that beat you → you CALL (you beat their bluffs), you don't raise.
cadv.strongValueCall: Value hand, but raising would mainly get you called by better: call.
cadv.drawCallImplied: Draw: {{eq}} equity + implied odds (you win more when you hit) → profitable call.
cadv.vulnDraw: ⚠️ Vulnerable draw (low flush or paired board): reverse implied odds — you can hit and still lose, play carefully.
cadv.bluffcatch: Bluff-catcher: your equity ({{eq}}) beats the odds ({{odds}}) → you call to beat their bluffs, but don't raise.
cadv.callProfit: Your equity ({{eq}}) beats the pot odds ({{odds}}) → profitable call, even without a big hand.
cadv.foldVulnDraw: Vulnerable draw too expensive: {{eq}} equity, but reverse implied odds (low flush / paired board, facing aggression) → you sometimes hit only to lose. You'd need {{req}}. Fold.
cadv.foldDraw: Draw too expensive: {{eq}} equity vs {{odds}} required{{turn}}. Fold.
cadv.oneCardLeft: — and only one card to come (little implied value)
cadv.foldMarginal: You just barely have the raw odds ({{eq}} vs {{odds}}), BUT {{dom}}{{multi}}{{aggr}}{{oop}}: you need a safety margin (~{{req}}) against reverse implied odds → marginal fold.
cadv.oopSuffix: and you don't close the action (out of position)
cadv.foldNoOdds: Equity ({{eq}}) below the odds ({{odds}}) and no draw: folding is most profitable.
cadv.protectionBet: PROTECTION / equity-denial bet: against a capped range you're the most likely hand AHEAD, but it's VULNERABLE (under-pairs → set, overcards → pair). You bet to END the hand and deny a free outdraw — not to get paid.
cadv.protectVsDraw: PROTECTION bet: DRAW-heavy board (flush/straight possible) and you're very likely AHEAD. Bet ~2/3 pot to charge the draws and deny them a free card — checking your strong hand here lets the flush/straight complete for free (that's exactly how you get outdrawn).
cadv.thinValue: Opponent range CAPPED (repeated checks): your pair beats most of their hands → THIN value. Bet SMALL (~1/3 pot) to get paid by worse pairs — a big bet would fold them out.
cadv.valueBet: Strong hand: bet for value and make the draws pay.
cadv.semiBluff: Strong draw: a semi-bluff wins the pot right away or by hitting.
cadv.onePairCheck: Medium pair: check to control the pot size and bluff-catch later.
cadv.bluffNoSdv: You have no showdown value ({{eq}}) → checking almost always gives up the pot.
cadv.bluffRepNuts: The opponent bet then checked (capped range) and the {{tex}} board favors YOUR in-position calling range: you rep the nuts → big fold equity. This is a bluff, not over-aggression.
cadv.checkSpeculative: Hand with no showdown value, but a bluff here would be speculative (board not credible, opponent not capped or out of position) → check.
cadv.checkWeak: Weak hand: check (a bluff is only profitable on the right boards and opponents).
cadv.fpWhySemi: small sizing + your draw → fold equity + outs if called.
